key_insight: Cross-platform proteomics of CSF and plasma from the Target ALS Global Natural History Study identified many ALS-related protein changes and showed that combining mass spectrometry with Olink can reveal complementary biomarker signals.
clinical_relevance: These findings could help accelerate the discovery of biofluid biomarkers and drug targets for ALS, which may improve patient stratification and future therapy development.
method: 35-plex TMTpro mass spectrometry on cerebrospinal fluid and plasma
Compared sporadic ALS cases with controls in the TALS GNHS cohort
Benchmarked TMTpro-MS findings against Olink proximity extension assay results
evidence_signal: The study analyzed 28 controls and 39 sporadic ALS samples in CSF, and 31 controls and 41 sporadic ALS samples in plasma, identifying 2,875 proteins in CSF and 1,118 in plasma with both shared and non-overlapping differentially expressed proteins across platforms.
watch_item: This is an initial cross-sectional study, so the protein changes are associative rather than proof of clinical utility or causal disease biology.
paper_title: The Target ALS Global Natural History Study: Cross-platform proteomics to accelerate biofluid biomarker and drug target discovery in amyotrophic lateral sclerosis
